note: Ground-truth set for the response-handler shouldRespond field. Hand-authored against the canonical evaluator description in packages/core/src/runtime/builtin-field-evaluators.ts: RESPOND when addressed directly or continuing a conversation, IGNORE when small-talk between others, STOP only on explicit termination requests. The agent name used below is 'Eliza' — fixtures phrase mentions accordingly.
originNote: Cases below are hand-authored and tagged as the manual probe set. A dataset-derived sibling at fixtures/should-respond.derived.json is the canonical regression measurement source (produced by scripts/derive-fixtures.mjs from packages/training/datasets/eliza1-sft-0_6b/test.jsonl).

cases.input: Hey, can you write me a quick summary of the JSON file I shared yesterday?
cases.notes: DM direct ask — always RESPOND.

cases.input: good morning
cases.notes: DM greeting — RESPOND in a DM channel.

cases.input: thanks, that was perfect
cases.notes: DM acknowledgement after task — RESPOND with brief reply.

cases.input: Eliza, can you remind me at 5pm to call the dentist?
cases.notes: Named directly in DM — RESPOND.

cases.input: stop, that's enough — please don't message me again about this
cases.notes: Explicit stop request — terminate.

cases.input: cancel that, I changed my mind
cases.notes: Cancellation of a task — RESPOND with acknowledgement, not STOP.

cases.input: yes please do that
cases.notes: Continuing an active exchange — RESPOND.

cases.input: Eliza, do you know what time the standup is?
cases.notes: Group channel, directly mentioned — RESPOND.

cases.input: @eliza could you summarize the thread above?
cases.notes: Group channel with @mention — RESPOND.

cases.input: Anyone know how to convert a PDF to markdown?
cases.notes: Open question to the room that Eliza can help with — RESPOND.

cases.input: Alice: anyone going to that ramen place for lunch? Bob: I'm in.
cases.notes: Logistical chat between two humans — IGNORE.

cases.input: Carol: but the new design is better. Dan: I disagree, it loses information density.
cases.notes: Two-person debate, no question, no mention — IGNORE.

cases.input: Alice: morning team! Bob: morning. Carol: good morning everyone.
cases.notes: Group greeting chatter — IGNORE.

cases.input: Bob: Eliza fixed my calendar issue yesterday, that was great.
cases.notes: Eliza mentioned in passing but the message is between humans about a past event — IGNORE.

cases.input: Alice: deployed the new build, looks good so far
cases.notes: Status update to the team, not addressed to Eliza — IGNORE.

cases.input: Eliza, stop responding to every message please — only when I tag you
cases.notes: Explicit stop request in a group channel — STOP.

cases.input: Eliza please draft a follow-up email to the design team about the launch
cases.notes: Explicit task request — RESPOND.

cases.input: lol
cases.notes: DM acknowledgement — RESPOND briefly.

cases.input: Eliza what's the weather going to be like tomorrow
cases.notes: Voice channel direct ask — RESPOND.

cases.input: okay yeah let me grab a coffee first
cases.notes: Voice channel overheard speech, not directed at Eliza — IGNORE.

cases.input: Eliza stop listening please
cases.notes: Voice stop request — STOP.

cases.input: and what about the second one?
cases.notes: Continuation question in an active DM thread — RESPOND.

cases.input: why is meeting culture like this
cases.notes: Rhetorical complaint in group chat — IGNORE.

cases.input: hey eliza, can you check whether the deploy hook fired this morning?
cases.notes: Lowercase mention but clearly addressed — RESPOND.

cases.input: ugh okay I really need to finish this thing
cases.notes: DM self-talk; in a DM channel Eliza should respond unless told otherwise.

cases.input: stop notifications for the next hour please
cases.notes: User asking to mute notifications — RESPOND to confirm, do not STOP the agent.

cases.input: and what time was that?
cases.notes: Follow-up after Eliza's last reply in the same thread — RESPOND.

cases.input: Alice: 🎂🎂🎂 Bob: happy birthday!!! Carol: 🎉
cases.notes: Birthday side-chat between humans — IGNORE.

cases.input: urgent: cancel the 4pm meeting and tell people I'll reschedule
cases.notes: Urgent task in DM — RESPOND immediately.

cases.input: umm
cases.notes: Voice partial / filler word — IGNORE so Eliza doesn't talk over the user.

cases.input: Eliza Cohen sent me an email earlier
cases.notes: DM channel with the name in body text (not addressing the agent), but in a DM channel Eliza still RESPOND-s by default.

cases.input: I read a thing about the old Eliza chatbot from MIT
cases.notes: Group channel — Eliza referenced as a topic, not addressed — IGNORE.
